enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on June 7, 2017, explaining a two-day gap in posting due to the hassle of his green-screen setup, then shares his plan to simplify by mounting green paper directly to the bathroom wall. He gives an update on his ongoing project of cataloging every classic book in history on his website — currently deep in Polybius — and excitedly previews a future video about using his phone's text-to-speech to listen to Wikipedia articles all day.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: current green-cloth setup is too cumbersome; switching to fixed green paper on bathroom wall will be simpler and higher quality
deeply motivated to catalog every classic book's page count and reading time; fascination with the scale keeps him going
most time-consuming entry in the project yet due to the number of books and chapters, but a major accomplishment when done
thinks it may be the best invention he has encountered; extremely excited about passive learning while going about daily life

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog for Wednesday, June 7th, 2017.
I think I took two days off because I'm having trouble motivating doing videos because I do my videos in my bathroom and I have to hang up a green screen, green cloth, and it takes time.
And I usually only do videos like late at night when I'm about to go to sleep and I'm just too lazy to put that thing up so I can do the video.
And I don't want to come out here because my roommate, who knows when my roommate is going to come out and distract the shit out of me.
So I need to, what I need to do is make it simpler.

And then I didn't want to attach it to anywhere and my room's filled up and I didn't want to attach it to anywhere like in my house because I didn't want to make my house look too cluttered.
But then, yeah, I decided I'm going to take it off the wall and put it on the outside of my door to my room and then the door to the bathroom.
So then I can, I can, because I have green paper that I bought and I think it's enough to put on the wall.
And so this way I just go in the bathroom and I just record.
Plus it's going to be a lot better quality.

It'll be perfect.
